items.title: [CRITICAL] Telegram Inbound Messages Not Logged Since February 2026
items.content:
## CRITICAL REGRESSION: Telegram Inbound Message Logging Broken

### Problem

**All Telegram inbound message entries in \openclaw.log\ are dated February 16, 2026.**

No Telegram inbound messages from March 26 (or any date after Feb 16) appear in the gateway log, despite active Telegram usage tonight (2026-03-26).

### Evidence

**Search query:** \Get-Content openclaw.log | Select-String \"telegram.*raw-update\"\

**Results:**
- ✅ February 16, 2026: 30+ Telegram message entries found
- ❌ March 26, 2026: **ZERO** Telegram message entries found

**Example entries (all from Feb 16):**
\\\
2026-02-16T07:57:58.879Z - telegram update: message_id:891 from 954092305: \"Your exec tool runs PowerShell 5.x...\"
2026-02-16T09:18:15.504Z - telegram update: message_id:932 from 954092305: \"run heartbeat.\"
2026-02-16T20:58:10.916Z - telegram update: message_id:1033 from 954092305: \"Which agent is on #59?\"
\\\

**No entries after February 16.**

### Impact

**~6 weeks of user commands have NO audit trail.**

This affects:
1. **Compliance** — No record of user commands for governance review
2. **Debugging** — Cannot trace what commands triggered what actions
3. **Security** — No audit log for security incident investigation
4. **Violations** — Cannot verify if/when violations occurred without command context

### Timeline

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| 2026-02-16 | Last logged Telegram inbound message |
| 2026-02-17 to 2026-03-26 | **NO Telegram inbound messages logged** |
| 2026-03-26 22:25 EDT | Regression discovered during Bug #157 investigation |

### Investigation Needed

1. **When did logging stop?** — Check if there's a specific commit/date
2. **Why did logging stop?** — Code change? Config change? Log rotation issue?
3. **Are messages being logged elsewhere?** — Check for separate Telegram log files
4. **Is this affecting other channels?** — Check WhatsApp, Discord inbound logging

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Root cause identified (commit/config/change that broke
items.author: seva

items.title: feat: add context-harvester platform tool with cron integration
items.content:
## Overview

This PR adds the Context Harvester tool — a TypeScript-based cron job that runs every 30 minutes to:

1. Scan active OpenClaw sessions
2. Extract recent messages
3. Send to DeepSeek for intelligent summarization
4. Generate a live CONTEXT.md snapshot briefing

## What's included

- **platform-tools/context-harvester/** — Complete tool with:
  - harvester.ts: Main script (session scanning, DeepSeek integration, retries)
  - run.sh: Executable wrapper
  - .env: API key configuration
  - Full README and tsconfig

- **patches/context-harvester-cron-config.patch** — Cron job config for Butley agents

- **CONTEXT-HARVESTER-CRON-CONFIG.md** — Complete deployment documentation

## How it works

The tool reads from ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/ JSONL files, extracts messages from the last 60 minutes, and sends them to DeepSeek Chat API along with the current CONTEXT.md. DeepSeek rewrites the entire file with only active topics — completed items naturally disappear on the next run.

**Benefits:**
- Live briefing: agents always know what's happening
- Concise: ~2-5KB snapshot, not an accumulating log
- Privacy: filters out private sessions
- Resilient: retries 3x on API failures, handles missing/empty state

## Testing

Currently running in production on main Gee agent (~/clawd/) — running successfully every 30 minutes, generating accurate context snapshots.

## Integration

1. Add cron config from patch to agent's openclaw.json
2. Set DEEPSEEK_API_KEY env var
3. Deploy
items.author: guiramos

items.title: Enhance MCP server integration and tool registration in OpenClaw
items.content:
## Description
This pull request introduces support for integrating external MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers as native tools within the OpenClaw Agent. It adds configuration options, validation, service registration, and runtime logic to discover, register, and manage tools exposed by MCP servers, making them available alongside built-in and native plugins. The changes also ensure robust configuration validation and proper resource management.

Key changes include:

**MCP Server Integration & Tool Registration**
- Added a new `McpServerToolRegistry` service that connects to configured MCP servers, discovers their available tools, and registers them as native tools in the `NativePluginRegistry`. This includes logic for both HTTP and stdio transports, tool name resolution, error handling, and resource cleanup. (`src/OpenClaw.Agent/Plugins/McpServerToolRegistry.cs`)
- Updated the dependency injection and runtime initialization to register and invoke the new MCP tool registry service, ensuring MCP tools are loaded at startup. (`src/OpenClaw.Gateway/Composition/ToolServicesExtensions.cs`, `src/OpenClaw.Gateway/Composition/RuntimeInitializationExtensions.cs`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-caf2a2f54fe44e2cdf883d4db50caffead4610240fa09aa8b5eceec43039d544R15-R18) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-0870a575064d5067d0a42d8d0dfb69debaf49e381f02c06b723a436b23e63e3cR65) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-0870a575064d5067d0a42d8d0dfb69debaf49e381f02c06b723a436b23e63e3cR123)

**Configuration and Validation Enhancements**
- Extended the plugin configuration model to support MCP servers, including transport, timeouts, environment variables, and tool name prefixing. (`src/OpenClaw.Core/Plugins/PluginModels.cs`, `src/OpenClaw.Gateway/appsettings.json`, `src/OpenClaw.Gateway/appsettings.Production.json`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-20d7514a94ac5f0edf461abde67b4f8de7df3dff321bba1537b7adf6f8fbb5e4R109-R137) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-1b83f97fb72ae671e362ae40a56e585e2d20897356e4075f7cb441ab19d66213R162-R173) [[3]]

items.title: Refresh calendar day modal after RSVP save
items.content:
Closes #403

## What changed
- added a focused Vitest regression for `calendar.html` that boots the page module, opens a day-detail modal containing both a DB-backed event and an ICS-only event, submits an RSVP, and verifies the modal stays open with refreshed button state and summary
- updated `calendar.html` to remember the active day-detail selection and rerender the open modal after a successful RSVP save in calendar view
- persisted run analysis notes for requirements, architecture, QA, and code plan under `docs/pr-notes/runs/issue-403-fixer-20260327T022502Z/`

## Why
The calendar RSVP flow updated in-memory event data but left the already-open day-detail modal stale, which could show the wrong selected state and outdated availability summary until the user closed and reopened it. This change adds regression coverage around that trust break and fixes the modal refresh with a minimal targeted patch.

## Validation
- `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run tests/unit/calendar-day-modal-rsvp.test.js`
- `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run tests/unit/calendar-rsvp.test.js tests/unit/calendar-page-cancellation.test.js`

## Summary

- Replaced OpenClaw-based AI context injection flow with direct AI configuration retrieval and refresh utilities.
- Refactored chat/config integration points across frontend and Tauri command surfaces to use the new configuration path.
- Simplified AI settings panel behavior around loading and refresh by aligning it to the updated config structure.
- Removed obsolete OpenClaw-specific loader/injector files and related initialization wiring.
- Added and enforced a repository PR template plus authoring guidance updates in docs/agent instructions.

## Problem

- The OpenClaw integration path added indirection and stale wiring across chat and settings flows, making AI configuration behavior harder to reason about and maintain.
- Reviewer consistency for PRs was also inconsistent due to missing standardized PR structure.

## Solution

- Introduced direct AI configuration retrieval/refresh handling in the Tauri/backend command path and updated frontend call sites accordingly.
- Updated impacted UI/state entry points (`Conversations`, `threadSlice`, `tauriCommands`, `AIPanel`) to consume the new flow.
- Deleted deprecated OpenClaw loader/injector modules and removed their startup usage.
- Added `.github/pull_request_template.md` and documented mandatory usage in project AI authoring docs.

力，将 zw 的早期技术进行了云端适配与 C 端场景的普及，是技术传承过程中的时代

items.author: m-f-vip

items.title: [Live updates] Task 3: Agent Updates End-to-End
items.author: fcarucci

items.title: Feature request: unified background task status dashboard
items.content:
## Summary

OpenClaw already supports async/background work across multiple execution mechanisms:

- main sessions
- sub-agents
- ACP sessions
- exec/process flows

But status visibility is still fragmented and too low-level.

Operators often have to mentally reconstruct task state from:

- sessions
- subagents
- process logs
- tool results
- runtime events

This makes background work technically observable, but not semantically clear.

---

## Problem

What users and agents actually want to know is:

- what is this task doing right now?
- is it blocked?
- why is it blocked?
- does it need intervention?
- is it done and just waiting to be reported?

Right now, too much of that has to be inferred manually.

---

## Proposed direction: unified task-centric status dashboard

Introduce a task-centric status model that is separate from raw sessions/processes.

Suggested per-task fields:

- task name
- owner / executor (main, subagent, ACP, process)
- current phase
- block reason
- latest meaningful update
- suggested next action
- last update time

---

## Suggested normalized phases

- `queued`
- `starting`
- `reading_context`
- `researching`
- `planning`
- `editing`
- `testing`
- `waiting_user`
- `waiting_approval`
- `waiting_dependency`
- `summarizing`
- `done`
- `failed`
- `stalled`

Suggested block reasons:

- waiting for user input
- waiting for confirmation
- waiting for approval
- waiting for OAuth / auth
- waiting for subagent
- waiting for external API
- suspected stall (no heartbeat / no new output)

---

## Important design point

A **task should not be treated as identical to a session**.

A single user-visible task may span:

- main session planning
- one or more sub-agent runs
- one or more exec processes
- retries
- final summarization back in the main session

So the model likely needs:

- `Task` = user intent unit
- `Run` = one execution attempt
- `Worker` = actual runtime/session/process

---

## What this should enable

A useful status dashboard shoul
items.author: upsightx

items.title: Feature request: unified execution planner for task routing
items.content:
## Summary

OpenClaw is powerful, but task routing is still too implicit.

For many requests, the agent has to infer too much before starting work:

- should this run in the main session?
- should this be delegated to a sub-agent?
- should this go through ACP?
- does this require a skill load first?
- does this require explicit confirmation?
- is this a channel-sensitive action with special reply rules?

All of this logic exists today, but it is distributed across prompts, skills, conventions, and operator experience.

That makes execution feel less consistent than it could be.

---

## Why this is a problem

This creates friction for both users and agents:

- more hesitation before execution
- more hidden policy complexity
- inconsistent tool/runtime selection
- more room for mistakes in sensitive operations
- higher mental overhead in multi-capability environments

In short, OpenClaw can do the right thing, but deciding *how to start* is often too implicit.

---

## Proposed direction: unified execution planner

Introduce a lightweight planning/routing layer that classifies the task before execution.

Example shape:

```json
{
  "mode": "direct",
  "reason": "simple local lookup",
  "risk": "low",
  "recommended_tools": ["read", "exec"],
  "skill_to_load": null,
  "requires_confirmation": false,
  "channel_policy": "reply_in_current_chat"
}
```

Useful dimensions:

- task kind: question / search / local edit / external write / long-running
- risk level: low / medium / high
- expected duration: seconds / minutes / background
- execution target: main / subagent / acp / confirm-first

---

## Expected behavior

The planner should ideally:

1. run automatically before execution
2. stay invisible for obvious cases
3. become visible when the route is ambiguous or high-risk
4. support user override
5. support agent reroute when task scope expands

Examples:

- simple local lookup → `direct`
- multi-file code task → `subagent`
- explicit “use codex / claude code / gemini”
items.author: upsightx

items.title: Feature request: unified execution planner and background task status dashboard
items.content:
## Summary

OpenClaw is powerful, but once you start using it heavily, two UX gaps become very obvious:

1. **Execution routing is too implicit**
2. **Background task visibility is too fragmented**

I'm opening this issue because these two problems create a lot of operator overhead for both users and agents.

---

## Problem 1: execution routing is implicit and mentally expensive

Today, a lot of task routing logic lives in prompts, skills, conventions, and agent experience.

For many requests, the agent has to implicitly decide:

- should this run in the main session?
- should this be delegated to a sub-agent?
- should this go through ACP?
- does this require a skill load first?
- does this require explicit confirmation?
- is this a channel-sensitive action with special reply rules?

The system *can* do all of these things, but the decision process is not explicit enough.

This creates friction:

- more hesitation before execution
- more hidden policy complexity
- more inconsistent tool selection
- more room for silent mistakes in channel-sensitive operations

### Proposed direction: unified execution planner

Introduce a lightweight planning/routing layer that classifies each task before execution.

Example output shape:

```json
{
  "mode": "direct",
  "reason": "simple local lookup",
  "risk": "low",
  "recommended_tools": ["read", "exec"],
  "skill_to_load": null,
  "requires_confirmation": false,
  "channel_policy": "reply_in_current_chat"
}
```

Useful dimensions:

- task kind: question / search / local edit / external write / long-running
- risk level: low / medium / high
- expected duration: seconds / minutes / background
- execution target: main / subagent / acp / confirm-first

This could start as:

- an internal planner used before execution
- a debug/inspection API or CLI (`openclaw route ...`)
- later a visible UI hint for explainability and override

---

## Problem 2: background tasks need a unified status dashboard

OpenClaw already supports async w

## Summary

- **Agent layer**: Rewrites all `AGENTS.md` prompts (main/material/order/planning) with a shell pipeline for UUID extraction (fixes LLM session key hallucination), idempotency sentinels, order→material callbacks, and trace events at every workflow step
- **New services**: `services/switch/` (Redis pub-sub WebSocket router) and `services/adaptor/` (IMAP poller that resumes paused agent sessions on email reply)
- **Frontend**: Always-expanded live trace panel with `workflowActive` keepalive; `business_id` injected into initial agent message; trace events handled from switch-service WebSocket
- **Security**: All hardcoded secrets moved to `${ENV_VAR}` refs; `.gitignore` updated to exclude all OpenClaw runtime directories

## What was verified end-to-end
1. Frontend sends Material event → main agent routes to material
2. Material runs `material_engine` → spawns order agent via `sessions_spawn`
3. Order runs `order_engine` (async) → HITL email sent on high impact
4. Human approves → IMAP adaptor resumes order session
5. Order sends notification + callback to material session
6. Material spawns planning agent
7. Planning runs `supply_chain_engine` → HITL email sent on high impact
8. Human approves → planning sends final notification → workflow complete
9. All 12+ trace steps visible in the UI in real time

items.author: yannonghuang

items.title: Bug: skillsSnapshot never refreshes after gateway restart (version counter resets to 0)
items.content:
## Summary

Long-lived sessions (e.g., the main Telegram session) retain stale skill entries indefinitely — even across gateway restarts. Skills that were renamed or deleted days ago still appear in the `available_skills` prompt, and new/replacement skills are missing.

## Root Cause

The skills snapshot version counter (`globalVersion` and `workspaceVersions` Map in `skills-remote-*.js`) is stored **in memory** and initialized to `0`. When the gateway restarts, these reset to `0`.

The refresh check in `session-updates-*.js` is:

```javascript
const shouldRefreshSnapshot = snapshotVersion > 0 
    && (nextEntry?.skillsSnapshot?.version ?? 0) < snapshotVersion;
```

After a restart:
- `snapshotVersion` = `getSkillsSnapshotVersion(workspaceDir)` = `Math.max(0, 0)` = **0**
- `shouldRefreshSnapshot` = `0 > 0 && ...` = **false** ← always false

The persisted snapshot also has `version: 0`, so the comparison never triggers a rebuild. The watcher works within a single gateway process lifetime (bumps version on SKILL.md add/change/unlink), but that state is lost on restart.

## Reproduction

1. Create a custom skill `~/.openclaw/skills/my-skill/SKILL.md`
2. Start the gateway, send a message (creates session with `skillsSnapshot.version: 0`)
3. Rename the skill folder to `my-new-skill`
4. Restart the gateway (`openclaw gateway restart`)
5. Send another message in the **same session**
6. The agent still sees `my-skill` in `available_skills` and does NOT see `my-new-skill`

Subagent sessions are unaffected because they get fresh sessions each time.

## Evidence

From a real deployment — `sessions.json` shows:

| Session | Skills count | Has stale `amazon-shop` | Has correct `online-shop` |
|---|---|---|---|
| `agent:main:main` (Telegram) | 16 | ✅ Yes (stale) | ❌ No |
| Recent subagents | 17 | ❌ No | ✅ Yes |

The `amazon-shop` skill was consolidated into `online-shop` days earlier. Multiple gateway restarts occurred in between. The gateway's live skill scanner (`openclaw skill

items.title: Consolidate plugins & skills into main repo + build Codex plugin
items.content:
## Context

Codex CLI now supports plugins ([docs](https://developers.openai.com/codex/plugins)) — installable bundles that can package skills, MCP servers, and app integrations.

**Our advantage:**
- Codex users are already our biggest traffic source (79 clicks/week on docs:/integrations/codex)
- "codex memory" is a top search query (pos 4.0, 30 clicks/week)
- We already have a working MCP server — plugin is just better packaging

## Current State (repo sprawl)

We have 3 separate repos that are really just different ways to distribute the same thing:

1. **basic-memory-skills** — 10 skills teaching agents how to use BM effectively
2. **basic-memory-plugins** — Claude Code plugin (skills + commands + hooks)
3. *Need:* **Codex plugin** — same skills, different packaging format

All three are **core BM functionality**, not separate products. They should live in the main repo.

## Proposed Unified Structure

```
basic-memory/
├── src/                        # Python MCP server + CLI (existing)
├── docs/                       # Docs (existing)
├── skills/                     # ← Merge basic-memory-skills here
│   ├── memory-tasks/
│   ├── memory-schema/
│   ├── memory-reflect/
│   ├── memory-notes/
│   ├── memory-defrag/
│   └── ... (10 total)
└── plugins/                    # ← Merge basic-memory-plugins here
    ├── claude-code/            # Claude Code plugin
    │   ├── .claude-plugin/
    │   ├── skills/             # Can reference ../skills/
    │   └── commands/
    └── codex/                  # New Codex plugin
        ├── .codex-plugin/
        ├── skills/             # Can reference ../skills/
        └── .mcp.json
```

## Why Consolidate

**Single source of truth:**
- Skills are core functionality, not a separate product
- Plugins are distribution mechanisms for those skills
- Easier to keep in sync with BM version bumps

**Avoid duplication:**
- Many skills are identical across Claude Code/Codex plugins
- Can reference or copy from central `/skills/` direct
items.author: bm-clawd

items.title: Feature Request: Per-agent MCP Server Configuration
items.content:
## Feature Request: Per-agent MCP Server Configuration

### Problem

Skills (技能) support per-agent configuration via the workspace/skills directory, but MCP Servers are loaded globally by the gateway and cannot be isolated per agent.

### Real-world Scenario

- User wants only a specific agent (e.g. ai-data-engineer) to access a particular MCP Server (e.g. ontoflow-jtbc)
- Other agents (e.g. main) do not need this MCP tool, but it still occupies token space in the context window (tool listing is included in context)
- Skills cannot replace MCP — MCP is a remote service, skills are local tools

### Expected Behavior

- Support per-agent MCP configuration like `agents.list[].mcpServers` similar to how skills work
- Or auto-load MCP config from `~/.openclaw/workspace-<agentId>/config/mcporter.json`

### Reference

Skills already have a per-agent design. MCP configuration could follow the same pattern.

### Additional Context

Currently:
- `~/.mcporter/mcporter.json` is loaded globally by the gateway
- `~/.openclaw/workspace-<agentId>/config/mcporter.json` is NOT auto-loaded
- Skills in `~/.openclaw/workspace-<agentId>/skills/` are per-agent, but mcporter skill only provides CLI access — it does not register MCP tools

This means MCP tools are always visible to all agents, which is not ideal for:
1. Token optimization (unnecessary tool lists in context)
2. Security/isolation (not all agents should access all MCP servers)
items.author: teng2-doudou

items.title: [Feature]: WebUI 区分“后台管理区”和“主交互区”
items.content:
## Summary

建议 CoPaw WebUI 区分“后台管理区”和“主交互区”，以提升聊天与任务执行的使用体验，同时为更多配置与信息透出提供空间。

## Component(s) Affected

- [x] Console (frontend web UI)

## Problem / Motivation

目前 WebUI 中，“聊天”作为主要使用场景，却只是左侧菜单中的一项。

同时，大量空间被配置、设置、文档入口等内容占据，导致：

- 主交互区域（聊天、任务执行）空间较小，体验不佳
- 用户在“使用 agent”和“配置系统”之间缺乏清晰区分
- 整体更像控制台，而不是面向使用的工作界面

对于以 WebUI 为主要入口的用户来说，这会影响可用性和理解成本。

## Proposed Solution

建议在信息架构上区分两类界面：

### 1. 后台管理区
用于：
- 技能、工具、MCP
- 运行配置、模型、环境变量等

### 2. 主交互区
用于：
- 聊天
- agent 执行过程
- 结果展示

可以考虑：

- 提供“管理模式 / 使用模式”切换
- 或将管理功能收纳到独立入口（类似后台页面）

此外：

- 顶栏中的“更新日志 / 文档 / 常见问题”等入口似乎指向同一站点，可以考虑收纳或简化，以释放主交互空间

## Alternatives Considered

- 保持当前结构，仅通过优化布局提升体验  
  → 但在不改变信息架构的前提下，改善空间和层级较有限

- 增加可折叠面板  
  → 有帮助，但仍无法解决“管理 vs 使用”混合的问题

## Additional Context

当前 UI 更接近控制台模式（类似 OpenClaw），但 CoPaw 同时也在强化 WebUI 使用场景，因此信息结构可能需要相应调整。

核心问题不在于功能多少，而在于：

**管理界面和使用界面没有被清晰区分。**

## Willing to Contribute

- [x] I am willing to open a PR for this feature (after discussion).
items.author: fujingzhai

items.title: refactor(kiloclaw): replace Linear CLI with Linear MCP server
items.content:
## Summary

After exploring other options for the Linear integration, the official Linear MCP server turned out to be a better fit than the CLI. All OpenClaw instances are already deployed with the `mcporter` package and skill, which give KiloClaw MCP abilities anyways. Importantly, unlike the CLI, mcporter also allows us to avoid ever writing our actual API key to a file, which significantly reduces the overhead for managing this integration. This PR targets feature/kiloclaw-linear-cli with the scoped changes to switch-over to this MCP integration only, to simplify the reviewer's experience.

## Changes

- Undo the changes to both `Dockerfile`s — remove `@schpet/linear-cli` install and move `xz-utils` cleanup earlier since the CLI's postinstall no longer needs it
- Strip all on-disk credential cleanup logic from `configureLinear()` — no files to manage anymore, so the function no longer needs `BootstrapDeps`
- Add Linear MCP server entry to `writeMcporterConfig()` when `LINEAR_API_KEY` is set; remove it when unset. Preserves existing user-configured servers
- Replace CLI usage documentation in the TOOLS.md agent section with a pointer to the Linear MCP server via mcporter
- Simplify `configureLinear` tests (no more fs/exec mocking) and add `writeMcporterConfig` tests covering add, remove, preserve, and multi-server scenarios

## Verification

- [x] `pnpm format:check` passes
- [x] `pnpm lint` passes
- [x] Tests pass (`vitest` in kiloclaw workspace)
- [x] E2E tested on a fresh instance

## Visual Changes

N/A

## Reviewer Notes

Net deletion — removes ~200 lines of CLI wrangling and credential cleanup in favor of a single MCP server config block.
items.author: evanjacobson

items.title: [提效想法 #3] 基于 Cline Kanban 多 Agent 编排 + OpenClaw 的并行代码审查流水线
items.content:
# 背景
Cline 于 2026-03-27 开源了 Cline Kanban，一个多 Agent 编排工具，支持任务并行化和 Kanban 风格的可视化管理。OpenClaw 支持通过 ACP 协议与其他 Agent 系统互操作。Claude Code 在 2026-03-27 新增了云端 auto-fix 功能，支持 PR 自动化修复。

当前痛点：
- 代码审查是串行的，单 Agent 审查大 PR 效率低
- 不同类型的审查（安全/性能/风格/逻辑）需要不同视角
- 审查结果需要人工汇总和分发

# 目标
构建一个基于多 Agent 并行编排的代码审查流水线：
1. PR 触发审查事件
2. 任务分发：并行启动多个专业 Agent（安全审查、性能审查、代码风格、逻辑审查）
3. Kanban 风格管理：每个审查任务独立状态（pending/in-progress/done）
4. 结果聚合：汇总各 Agent 审查意见，生成统一审查报告
5. 可选：自动 fix（Claude Code auto-fix 能力）

架构参考：Cline Kanban 的多 Agent 编排 + OpenClaw 的 ACP 协议 + Claude Code auto-fix

# 子任务
- [ ] 研究 Cline Kanban 的多 Agent 编排机制和 API
- [ ] 设计审查流水线架构：触发器→分发器→并行 Agent→聚合器
- [ ] 实现 OpenClaw MCP 工具：pr-review-trigger、review-aggregator
- [ ] 实现 3 个专业审查 Agent（安全/性能/风格）
- [ ] 集成 Claude Code auto-fix 作为可选自动修复层
- [ ] 测试：用真实 PR 验证并行审查效果

# 预期收益
- **效率提升**：并行审查比串行节省 50-70% 时间
- **覆盖率提升**：多 Agent 同时覆盖安全/性能/风格/逻辑多维度
- **自动化**：从 PR 触发到审查报告全流程自动

# 实施难度
**高** — 涉及多 Agent 架构设计、ACP 协议对接、Claude Code auto-fix API 集成
预计工时：2-3 天
items.author: ThomasLiu

items.title: [提效想法 #2] Claude Code /init 交互式引导 + OpenClaw 自动化初始化工作流
items.content:
# 背景
Claude Code 正在测试新版 /init 功能（2026-03-23），引入交互式机制，通过提问引导用户生成 Claude.MD 并定制 Skills 和 Hooks。同时 OpenClaw 也支持 skills 和 hooks 系统。

当前痛点：
- 新代码库初始化依赖人工配置 Agent（CLAUDE.md、skills、hooks）
- 不同项目类型（全栈/前端/Node.js/嵌入式）需要不同配置模板
- 每次新建项目都要重复手动配置

# 目标
基于 Claude Code /init 的交互式引导理念，为 OpenClaw 构建一个自动化初始化工作流：
1. 扫描代码库（package.json、tsconfig、框架类型等）
2. 自动生成 CLAUDE.md（项目说明、架构、技术栈）
3. 推荐并安装匹配的 Skills（参考 MiniMax Skills）
4. 配置合适的 Hooks（pre-commit、CI 检查等）
5. 输出一个完整的"AI Ready"项目

# 子任务
- [ ] 设计 OpenClaw 项目扫描器（检测 Node.js 全栈项目的关键文件）
- [ ] 设计交互式引导流程（类比 Claude Code /init）
- [ ] 实现 CLAUDE.md 自动生成器
- [ ] 实现 Skills 自动推荐与安装
- [ ] 实现 Hooks 自动配置（git hook、MCP 集成）
- [ ] 测试：拿真实项目验证初始化效果

# 预期收益
- **效率提升**：新项目初始化从 15 分钟→ 1 分钟
- **一致性**：所有项目保持统一的 AI 配置标准
- **可发现性**：强制生成 CLAUDE.md，让 Agent 更快理解项目

# 实施难度
**低-中** — OpenClaw 的 skills/hooks 已有基础设施，核心是工作流编排
预计工时：0.5-1 天
items.author: ThomasLiu

items.title: [提效想法 #1] 基于 MiniMax Skills 仓库 + OpenClaw per-agent reasoning 的自适应 Skill 推荐引擎
items.content:
# 背景
MiniMax AI 于 2026-03-23 开源了 Skills 仓库，涵盖前端、全栈、移动端原生开发等九大核心技能，可集成至 Claude Code、Cursor、Codex、OpenCode 及 OpenClaw。同期 OpenClaw v2026.3.22 新增了 per-agent reasoning 推理功能。

当前问题：Skill 的选择依赖人工判断，Agent 无法根据任务上下文自动选择最优 Skill，导致：
- 通用 Agent 在专业任务上表现平庸
- 手动配置 Skill 增加认知负担
- 不同任务反复切换 Skill 效率低

# 目标
构建一个 Skill 推荐引擎，让 OpenClaw Agent 在任务开始时自动分析代码库特征、任务类型、上下文，动态推荐最匹配的 Skills，将 Skill 使用从手动配置升级为智能自适应。

# 子任务
- [ ] 分析 MiniMax Skills 仓库的 9 个 Skill 的能力边界和适用场景
- [ ] 设计代码库特征提取器（语言、框架、文件结构、依赖）
- [ ] 设计任务类型分类器（前端/后端/全栈/DevOps/数据分析等）
- [ ] 实现 OpenClaw Skill 推荐插件（Hook 或 MCP 工具）
- [ ] 测试推荐准确率：给定任务描述+代码库，能否正确推荐相关 Skill
- [ ] 评估与 per-agent reasoning 的结合方式

# 预期收益
- **效率提升**：减少人工选 Skill 的时间，每次任务节省 2-5 分钟
- **质量提升**：专业任务匹配专业 Skill，生成质量更高
- **可扩展**：未来可接入更多 Skill 源（ClawHub、MCP 等）

# 实施难度
**中等** — 需要：理解 OpenClaw Hook/MCP 机制，设计轻量推荐逻辑
预计工时：1-2 天
items.author: ThomasLiu

items.title: [Question]: 建议 WebUI 区分“后台管理区”和“主交互区”
items.content:
## Question or topic

建议 CoPaw 的 WebUI 区分 **后台管理区** 和 **主交互区**，而不是把“聊天”仅作为左侧菜单中的一项。

## Context

目前 CoPaw 的 WebUI 基本继承自 OpenClaw，但我感觉它面临一个比较明显的问题：

- “聊天”其实才是真正的主要使用场景
- 但在当前界面里，它只是整个控制台菜单中的一项
- 大量空间被配置、设置、文档入口等内容占据，导致聊天区域比较逼仄，使用体验不够好

如果 OpenClaw 更鼓励用户使用 TUI 和通信渠道，那么这种控制台式布局还可以理解；  
但 CoPaw 明显也希望用户直接使用 WebUI，那么我认为就更有必要把：

- 后台管理区（配置、技能、工具、MCP、运行设置等）
- 主交互区（聊天、任务执行、结果查看）

做更明确的分层或切换。

一个比较自然的思路，是参考常见后台/前台结构：

- 通过一个按钮或入口切换“管理模式”和“使用模式”
- 管理模式承载更多配置与信息透出
- 使用模式则把主要空间留给聊天、任务过程和结果展示

这样一来：
- 可以提升 agent 聊天和实际使用体验
- 也能给后续路线图中提到的“透出更多信息和配置”留出空间

另外，当前顶栏里的“更新日志 / 文档 / 常见问题”等入口都指向同一目标网站，感觉没有必要长期占据这么多顶部空间，也进一步压缩了主交互区域。

## Tried so far

我对当前 UI 做了一些观察，也结合 agent 产品的使用习惯想了下，感觉目前最核心的问题不是“功能不够多”，而是：

**管理界面和使用界面没有被清晰地区分。**

如果这个方向是符合项目设计思路的，也许可以考虑后续逐步调整 WebUI 的信息架构。

items.title: Align ACPX built-in agent registry with latest acpx
items.content:
## Summary

- Problem: `#43499` added Cursor to OpenClaw's ACPX-facing allowlist/docs, but the branch drifted behind current `openclaw/acpx` built-in agent semantics.
- Why it matters: rebasing the old branch as-is would preserve stale ACPX defaults like `cursor -> agent acp` and keep OpenClaw's ACPX mirror incomplete.
- What changed: rebased on current `main`, synced the OpenClaw-side ACPX built-in agent registry/docs to current `openclaw/acpx`, and expanded coverage beyond Cursor to the newer built-ins.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no ACP runtime architecture changes, no non-ACPX agent routing changes, no unrelated test/CI work.

items.title: refactor: runtime.py 5-module OpenClaw-style decomposition
items.content:
## Summary
- **`core/runtime.py`** 1476 -> 517 lines: extracted wiring logic into `core/runtime_wiring/` package (4 sub-modules)
- `bootstrap.py` (345 lines): hooks, memory, session store, config watcher, task graph, prompt assembler, plugin registry
- `infra.py` (228 lines): policies, tool registry, LLM adapters, auth profiles, lane queue
- `automation.py` (261 lines): L4.5 9 components + hook wiring (table-driven logger hooks reduce 127 -> ~45 lines)
- `adapters.py` (243 lines): MCP signal/notification/calendar/gateway adapters
- Backward-compatible re-exports: `_build_default_policies`, `_build_default_registry`, `_build_default_lanes`, `_make_run_log_handler`, `get_plugin_status` remain importable from `core.runtime`
- Updated `test_memory_writeback.py` to import `build_automation` directly from wiring module

## Why
runtime.py was a 1476-line god module handling hooks, memory, LLM, tools, automation, MCP adapters, and gateway wiring. OpenClaw plugin pattern splits wiring by concern area, making each module independently readable and testable. No behavior change.

## Test plan
- [x] `uv run ruff check core/ tests/` -- 0 errors

items.author: Copilot

items.title: feat: standalone MCP skills server for waoowaoo video production workflow
items.content:
The core video production workflow (novel → script → storyboard → images → voice → video) needed to be exposed as a standalone skills package usable by MCP clients like openClaw — not embedded inside the Next.js app.

## What's new: `skills/`

Independent Node.js package at repo root. No Next.js, no Prisma — direct MySQL access via `mysql2` + `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk@1.26.0` over stdio transport.

**Three tools:**
- `list_projects` — paginated project list scoped to `WAOOWAOO_USER_ID`
- `get_project_workflow_status` — queries all 11 production stages, returns per-stage completion flags, overall `progressPercent`, and a Chinese-language `nextStep` recommendation
- `create_project` — inserts `projects` + `novel_promotion_projects` rows

**Config (`skills/.env`):**
```
DATABASE_URL=******localhost:3306/waoowaoo
WAOOWAOO_USER_ID=<id from users table>
```

**openClaw / Claude Desktop MCP config:**
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "waoowaoo": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["/path/to/waoowaoo/skills/dist/index.js"],
      "env": {
        "DATABASE_URL": "mysql://...",
        "WAOOWAOO_USER_ID": "..."
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Reverted

Previous session erroneously embedded a `novel-production` skill inside the existing `assistant-platform`. Those changes (types, registry, system-prompts, chat route, tests) have been fully reverted — the main app is back to its original state.

# Personal AI Agent Ecosystem Digest: Cross-Project Analysis
**Date:** 2026-03-27
**Analyst:** Senior Ecosystem Observer

## 1. Ecosystem Overview
The open-source personal AI agent ecosystem is currently undergoing a phase of rapid architectural maturation, shifting from simple chatbots to autonomous, multi-platform agents. We are observing a clear bifurcation in technical approaches: **TypeScript/Rust-based platforms** (e.g., OpenClaw, LoongClaw, ZeptoClaw) are prioritizing performance, security (SSRF protection), and local-first capabilities, while **Python-based frameworks** (e.g., CoPaw) are focusing on extensive model compatibility and complex agentic workflows (Skills/MCPs). A primary focus across the landscape is the integration of **reasoning/thinking models** and the stabilization of **memory/context management** systems to prevent hallucination during long tasks. # AI 智能体开源生态横向对比分析报告 (2026-03-27)

**分析师**: AI 开源生态技术观察员
**数据基准日**: 2026-03-27

---

## 1. 生态全景
当前个人 AI 助手与自主智能体开源生态正处于**架构重构与体验升级的关键分水岭**。
*   **去依赖化趋势明显**：头部项目（如 NanoBot）正激进剥离第三方中间件（如 `litellm`），转而采用原生 SDK 以解决安全审计与兼容性痛点。
*   **多模态与记忆成标配**：长期记忆（Mem0/Graphiti）和多模态交互（语音/视觉）已成为核心竞争力，社区不再满足于简单的对话补全。
*   **稳定性危机伴随爆发式增长**：虽然各项目活跃度极高，但大规模重构导致了 API 挂起、编码错误和配置失效等连锁反应，生态整体处于“快速迭代但不完全稳定”的状态。

→ https://www.theatlantic.com/technology/2026/03/ai-boom-polycrisis/686559/

**2. GitHub Copilot 数据训练：4 月 24 日起默认开启（The Register）**  
Copilot Free/Pro/Pro+ 用户交互数据（含代码片段、聊天内容、文件结构）将默认用于 AI 训练，Business/Enterprise 用户除外。用户需手动关闭设置选项。评论区 59 票反对 vs 3 票支持。  
→ https://www.theregister.com/2026/03/26/github_ai_training_policy_changes/

**3. ICML 会议用 AI 水印揪出论文作弊者（Semafor）**  
国际机器学习大会在审稿中埋入隐藏水印，诱导 AI 工具生成特定暗语，成功识别约 2% 论文存在违规 AI 辅助审稿，相关论文已被拒稿。另有 2025 年某会议发现 21% 同行评审疑似 AI 生成。  
→ https://www.semafor.com/article/03/26/2026/ai-conference-rejects-papers-over-ai-use

**4. Cognition 深度揭秘：Devin 背后的极限创业文化（SF Standard）**  
从阿瑟顿豪宅搬至南公园新总部；私人厨师全天候供餐；凌晨 3 点下班是常态；收购 Windsurf 后大换血。CEO Scott Wu 认为 Devin 是"让每个人都有实现想法的伙伴"而非取代工程师。公司估值超 100 亿美元，年化收入 25 亿美元，仍在亏损。  
→ https://sfstandard.com/2026/03/24/grind-sf-startup-racing-build-ai-software-engineer/

**5. JSONata 重写：AI 花 7 小时 / $400，省 500 万美元/年（Reco.ai）**  
用 AI 辅助从零重写 JSONata 管道（JSON 转换语言），得到纯 Go 实现 gnata。将表达式分为快速路径（字节扫描）和完整路径（AST 解析），结合批量流式求值，移除整个 RPC 集群，最终节省约 500 万美元/年。  
→ https://www.reco.ai/blog/we-rewrote-jsonata-with-ai

**6. Palantir 争议：纽约市医院系统宣布不续约（The Guardian）**  
纽约市公共医院系统将在 10 月合同到期后终止与 Palantir 近 400 万美元的合作。Palantir 接触患者健康记录并保留"去标识化数据用于研究以外目的"的权利。同时其在英国 NHS 的 3.3 亿英镑合同正面临隐私维权组织的强力抵制。  
→ https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/mar/26/new-york-hospitals-palantir-ai

**7. Open Source vs. Closed Source AI：真正的问题不是技术，是钱（Dave Friedman）**  
提出"可变现差距（Monetizable Spread）"概念：开源与闭源模型能力差距已压缩至约 3 个月，但大部分 AI 收入来自"足够好"就能胜任的任务，开源正在蚕食这个中间
items.author: urzz

items.title: 💡 跨平台桌面 AI 代理协作调度平台
items.content:
## 背景
本周 AI 领域最重大的进展是 **AI 代理桌面控制能力** 的爆发：
- Anthropic 的 Claude 现在可以直接控制用户的 Mac，点击、打字、导航软件
- OpenClaw 开源生态爆发，催生了 NemoClaw、Coasty 等一系列 "claws" 工具
- Claude Dispatch 功能让用户可以从手机远程调度桌面 AI 代理执行任务

然而，当前这些方案都是孤立的：
- 每个平台只能运行单一 AI 代理
- 代理之间无法协作
- 调度和监控能力有限

## 解决方案
构建一个 **跨平台桌面 AI 代理协作调度平台**：

### 核心功能
1. **多代理编排引擎** - 在同一桌面运行多个专业 AI 代理（代码、设计、数据等），支持任务分发和结果汇总
2. **远程调度中心** - 手机/网页端统一调度，支持定时任务、条件触发、工作流编排
3. **代理市场** - 社区共享可复用的代理能力模块（如 "自动整理邮件"、"生成周报"、"代码审查"）
4. **安全沙箱** - 代理操作隔离、权限控制、操作审计日志

### 技术栈建议
- 基础层：OpenClaw 开源框架
- 编排层：DAG 工作流引擎
- 调度层：WebSocket + Push 通知
- 安全层：macOS App Sandbox + Linux containers

### 差异化优势
- 相比 Claude Cowork：开放生态，不绑定单一模型
- 相比 OpenClaw：增加多代理协作和远程调度
- 相比 Coasty：社区驱动的代理市场

## 可行性
⭐⭐⭐⭐ (4/5)

**优势：**
- OpenClaw 已验证技术可行性
- 市场需求明确（Claude Dispatch 50% 成功率仍有大量用户）
- 开源社区可加速迭代

**挑战：**
- 跨平台兼容性（macOS/Linux/Windows）
- 安全性审核和用户信任建立
- 与各 AI 模型厂商的 API 集成

**MVP 建议：** 先聚焦 macOS + 单一模型（如 Claude），验证核心价值后再扩展。

---
*灵感来源：VentureBeat 2026-03-24 报道 - Anthropic Claude Mac 控制功能发布*

items.title: Config: plumb opt-in SSRF policy for web fetch, citation redirects, and remote media
items.content:
## Summary
This PR converts the previously proven dist-level SSRF benchmark-range hotfix into a source-level, config-driven, opt-in implementation.

It adds shared `ssrfPolicy` config plumbing for three scoped paths:

- `tools.web.fetch.ssrfPolicy`
- `tools.web.search.citationRedirect.ssrfPolicy`
- `messages.remoteMedia.ssrfPolicy`

It also fixes legacy web search normalization so `tools.web.search.citationRedirect` is preserved instead of being dropped during normalization.

## Why
Some public endpoints can resolve into RFC 2544 benchmarking / special-use address space and were being blocked as false positives by SSRF handling.

The goal here is **not** to broaden defaults or add domain-specific exceptions, but to expose existing shared SSRF policy controls in the right runtime/config surfaces so operators can opt in per subsystem.

## What changed
- Added shared SSRF policy schema/type reuse for:
  - `tools.web.fetch.ssrfPolicy`
  - `tools.web.search.citationRedirect.ssrfPolicy`
  - `messages.remoteMedia.ssrfPolicy`
- Threaded fetch SSRF policy into the `web_fetch` guarded fetch path
- Threaded citation redirect SSRF policy into Gemini citation redirect resolution
- Threaded remote media SSRF policy into outbound remote media loading paths
- Preserved `citationRedirect` during legacy web-search normalization
- Added focused tests for:
  - guarded fetch SSRF policy forwarding
  - citation redirect SSRF policy forwarding
  - legacy normalization retention
  - outbound media option passthrough

## Non-goals
- No broad hardcoded allowlists
- No domain-specific exceptions
- No Discord reconnect/lifecycle changes
- No unrelated refactors

## Validation
Scoped validation performed:
- `src/agents/tools/web-guarded-fetch.test.ts`
- `src/agents/tools/web-search.redirect.test.ts`
- `src/config/legacy-web-search.test.ts`
- `src/media/load-options.test.ts`
- `src/plugin-sdk/outbound-media.test.ts`
- `pnpm config:schema:check`

items.author: willsupernormal

items.title: [Feature]: Add a config-backed Tlon send surface
items.content:
## Surface

Channels / integrations

## Problem statement

LoongClaw still treats Tlon as a catalog-only planned surface. Operators can see `tlon-send` and `tlon-serve` in the catalog metadata, but they cannot configure a real account-aware outbound send path, validate the required Tlon credentials, or inspect readiness through the shared channel inventory and doctor flow.

## Proposed solution

Promote Tlon from a planned catalog stub to a shipped config-backed outbound surface.

The first iteration should:

- add a real `TlonChannelConfig` account model with env-pointer defaults for the existing `ship`, `url`, and `code` contract
- wire Tlon into the shared channel SDK and registry snapshot builders
- implement `tlon-send` as a config-backed outbound command with correct target-kind validation and transport/result validation
- update daemon CLI wiring, docs, inventory metadata, and tests so Tlon behaves like the other shipped config-backed outbound channels

## Non-goals / out of scope

- Do not ship `tlon-serve` in this slice
- Do not add richer Tlon thread-sync or inbound runtime ownership in this slice
- Do not introduce a new plugin system or alternate channel abstraction just for Tlon

## Alternatives considered

- Keep Tlon as catalog-only planned metadata. Rejected because it preserves catalog drift and leaves one of the remaining openclaw-aligned channel surfaces unshipped.
- Ship both `tlon-send` and `tlon-serve` together. Rejected because the runtime contract is materially larger and would blur the smallest correct slice.
- Promote `zalo-personal` first. Rejected because the openclaw-aligned personal-account surface is based on unofficial automation with account-ban risk, while Tlon is a cleaner next shipped surface.

## Acceptance criteria

- `LoongClawConfig` includes a real `tlon` channel config with account-aware resolution and env-pointer defaults
- `loongclaw tlon-send` exists and is wired through the daemon CLI
- the channel registry reports Tlon

## Summary

This PR adds a complete end-to-end test suite for the Cross daemon, including mock servers for upstream LLMs (Anthropic, OpenAI), gate LLM, Slack, and SMTP. The tests verify the full request flow through the proxy, gating decisions, notifications, and dashboard functionality.

## Key Changes

- **`tests/e2e/conftest.py`**: Core test fixtures that:
  - Spin up mock upstream LLM servers (Anthropic and OpenAI format) on ephemeral TCP ports
  - Start a mock gate LLM server for denylist + LLM review gate testing
  - Launch the real Cross daemon with mocked settings and isolated state
  - Provide httpx clients for making requests through the proxy
  - Include helper functions (`send_anthropic_message`, `send_openai_message`, `call_gate_api`, `register_session`, `end_session`) for common test operations
  - All network I/O stays in-process via ASGI transports (no real network calls)

- **`tests/e2e/mock_servers.py`**: Lightweight Starlette/ASGI mock implementations:
  - `MockAnthropicServer`: Fake Anthropic Messages API with SSE streaming and tool_use responses
  - `MockOpenAIServer`: Fake OpenAI Chat Completions API with function calling support
  - `MockGateLLMServer`: Mock gate LLM for testing review decisions
  - `MockSlackAPI`: Captures Slack API calls
  - `MockSMTP`: Captures sent emails

- **`tests/e2e/test_claude_code.py`**: Tests for Claude Code agent flow:
  - Session lifecycle (register, end)
  - Proxy passthrough for text responses (streaming and non-streaming)
  - Denylist gating (safe tools allowed, dangerous tools blocked)

- **`tests/e2e/test_openclaw.py`**: Tests for OpenClaw agent flow:

items.title: Bug: message send with local media on openclaw-weixin is misclassified as poll creation
items.content:
## Summary

When using the `message` tool with `action="send"` and a local file path in `media` for `channel="openclaw-weixin"`, OpenClaw rejects the request before it reaches the channel plugin with:

```text
Poll fields require action "poll"; use action "poll" instead of "send".
```

This appears to be a message normalization / validation bug in core, not a Weixin runtime send failure.

## Environment

- OpenClaw: `2026.3.13`
- Channel: `openclaw-weixin`
- Account: `4b4f3c849bb9-im-bot`
- Runtime: macOS / Node 22.22.0

## Repro

Using the `message` tool (or equivalent internal message send path), send a local file:

- `action`: `send`
- `channel`: `openclaw-weixin`
- `media`: `/Users/js/Desktop/docs/openclaw-guides/weixin-multi-agent-guide-2026-03-27.pdf`
- empty `message`

Observed error:

```text
Poll fields require action "poll"; use action "poll" instead of "send".
```

I reproduced the same behavior with:
- `.md`
- `.pdf`

So this is not file-type specific.

## Evidence this happens before plugin send

Gateway log shows:

```text
[tools] message failed: Poll fields require action "poll"; use action "poll" instead of "send".
```

The Weixin channel itself is healthy/running. The request is blocked before outbound plugin handling.

## Suspected cause

Core message validation contains:

```js
if (action === "send" && hasPollCreationParams(params))
  throw new Error('Poll fields require action "poll"; use action "poll" instead of "send".');
```

The tool bridge appears to be passing poll-related default fields on ordinary sends (e.g. empty / false / zero poll fields), and one of them is being treated as present.

This causes regular file sends to be misclassified as poll creation.

## Expected behavior

`action="send"` with `media` set to a local file path should proceed as a normal outbound media/file send for `openclaw-weixin`.

At minimum, empty/default poll fields should not cause send requests to be rejected.

## Suggested fix

One of:

1. In core, only trea

items.title: feat: split plugin into manifest (local) and manifest-provider (cloud)
items.content:
## Summary

Splits the monolithic `openclaw-plugin` into two independently publishable packages under `packages/openclaw-plugins/`:

- **`manifest`** (npm: `manifest`) — full self-hosted plugin with embedded NestJS server, SQLite, and dashboard (~50MB). Always released when backend/frontend code changes.
- **`manifest-provider`** (npm: `manifest-provider`) — lightweight cloud-only provider plugin (~22KB). Registers `manifest/auto` model with interactive auth onboarding via OpenClaw's Provider Plugin SDK. Only released when its own code changes.

### Why

Cloud users (the primary audience) were downloading ~50MB for a 22KB plugin. The embedded server, SQLite database, and frontend dashboard are only needed for local/self-hosted mode.

### What changed

**New provider plugin features:**
- `registerProvider()` with `ProviderPlugin` type — interactive auth onboarding via `openclaw providers setup manifest-provider`
- `providerAuthEnvVars` for `MANIFEST_API_KEY` env var detection
- Static model catalog with `auto` model
- Subscription provider discovery

**Package split:**
- `packages/openclaw-plugins/manifest-provider/` — cloud-only: auth, tools, commands, subscription sync
- `packages/openclaw-plugins/manifest/` — self-contained: embedded NestJS + SQLite + dashboard

**CI/CD:**
- Separate test jobs for each plugin
- Changeset enforcement: backend/frontend changes require `manifest` changeset; provider changes require `manifest-provider` changeset
- Release workflow loops through published packages for GitHub releases
- Codecov flags for both packages

**Docs:** Updated CLAUDE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, SKILL.md, codecov.yml, eslint.config, turbo.json

## Test plan

- [ ] `npm test --workspace=packages/openclaw-plugins/manifest-provider` — 10 suites, 205 tests
- [ ] `npm test --workspace=packages/openclaw-plugins/manifest` — 1 suite, 8 tests
- [ ] TypeScript compiles clean for both packages
- [ ] `manifest-provider` builds to ~22KB bundle
- [ ] `manifest` builds entry to ~5KB
items.author: brunobuddy

items.title: [Feature]: Expose messageId in message_sent plugin hook (toPluginMessageSentEvent)
items.content:
## Summary

The `message_sent` plugin hook does not expose the native `messageId` returned by the channel (WhatsApp/Telegram) after sending a message. The value exists internally in the `canonical` object but is filtered out by `toPluginMessageSentEvent()` before reaching plugins.

## Current behavior

```javascript
// toPluginMessageSentEvent returns:
{
  to: canonical.to,
  content: canonical.content,
  success: canonical.success,
  // messageId is NOT included
}
```

## Expected behavior

```javascript
{
  to: canonical.to,
  content: canonical.content,
  success: canonical.success,
  messageId: canonical.messageId,  // ← native channel message ID
}
```

## Use case

Plugins that archive messages (e.g., a `message-archive` plugin that saves all inbound/outbound to PostgreSQL) need the native message ID to:
- Track messages by their WhatsApp/Telegram ID
- Correlate outbound messages with inbound replies
- Avoid generating synthetic IDs

## Workaround

We currently patch the compiled code manually:

```bash
# In toPluginMessageSentEvent, add messageId to the returned object
sed -i 's/success: canonical.success,/success: canonical.success, messageId: canonical.messageId,/' dist/hook-runtime-*.js
```

This works but must be reapplied on every OpenClaw update.

## Environment

- OpenClaw 2026.3.23-2
- Affected files: multiple chunks containing `toPluginMessageSentEvent` (auth-profiles-*.js, discord-*.js, model-selection-*.js, reply-*.js, hook-runtime-*.js)
items.author: marieldejesus12

items.title: fix(feishu): read full CardKit streaming card content via raw_card_content param
items.content:
## Problem

When reading messages that were sent as CardKit streaming cards (created via `POST /cardkit/v1/cards`), the IM API (`GET /im/v1/messages/{message_id}`) returns fallback content (a screenshot image + placeholder text like `[Interactive Card]`) instead of the actual card text content. This makes it impossible to retrieve the full text of bot replies that use streaming cards.

## Root Cause

CardKit streaming cards are referenced by `card_id` in the message body (`{"type":"card","data":{"card_id":"xxx"}}`). The CardKit API only provides write endpoints — there is no read API to fetch card content by `card_id`. However, the IM message read API supports an undocumented query parameter `card_msg_content_type=raw_card_content` that instructs the API to inline the full card structure as a `json_card` field in the response content.

## Fix

### 1. Pass `card_msg_content_type` parameter

Add `card_msg_content_type: "raw_card_content"` to both:
- `client.im.message.get()` — used by `getMessageFeishu()` for single message reads
- `client.im.message.list()` — used by `listFeishuThreadMessages()` for thread history

### 2. Parse `json_card` format in `parseInteractiveCardContent`

Before the existing element-based parsing, check for the `json_card` field (a JSON string) and extract text from:
- `config.summary.content` — the card summary (streaming cards write the full reply text here)
- `body.elements` — recursively extract `plain_text` and `markdown` nodes, including nested structures like `column_set`

Falls through to the existing `elements` / `body.elements` parsing if `json_card` is absent or unparseable.

### 3. Tests

Added 3 new test cases:
- Verifies `card_msg_content_type` param is passed to `message.get`
- Verifies `json_card` format is correctly parsed (summary + nested body elements)
- Verifies fallback to legacy elements when `json_card` contains invalid JSON

## Related Issues

- Relates to #54972 (CardKit streaming card content not readable)

items.author: SebConejo

items.title: fix(doctor): resolve false positives in hook script validation
items.content:
## Description

### Context

While running `/octo:doctor` during a routine session health check, 5 hook script validation failures appeared in the `[hooks]` category — even though all 32 hook scripts exist on disk and are correctly executable. The doctor was reporting false positives, which undermines trust in the diagnostics output.

### Root cause

The hook validation logic in `scripts/lib/doctor.sh` (line 509) extracts the script path from each hook command using:

```bash
script_path=$(echo "$resolved_path" | awk '{print $1}')
```

This takes the **first whitespace-delimited token** as the file path. However, `hooks.json` contains two command patterns where the first token is NOT the script path:

**Pattern 1 — `bash`-wrapped commands** (2 occurrences):
```json
{ "command": "bash \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/strategy-rotation.sh\"" }
{ "command": "bash \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/done-criteria.sh\"" }
```
`awk '{print $1}'` extracts `bash` → file existence check fails → false positive.

**Pattern 2 — env-var prefixed commands** (3 occurrences):
```json
{ "command": "CLAUDE_HOOK_EVENT=SessionStart ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/telemetry-posthog.sh" }
{ "command": "CLAUDE_HOOK_EVENT=SessionEnd ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/telemetry-posthog.sh" }
{ "command": "CLAUDE_HOOK_EVENT=Stop ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/telemetry-posthog.sh" }
```
`awk '{print $1}'` extracts the env-var assignment (e.g. `CLAUDE_HOOK_EVENT=SessionStart`) → file existence check fails → false positive.

### All 5 false positives

| Command in hooks.json | Token extracted (wrong) | Actual script |
|---|---|---|
| `bash "${...}/strategy-rotation.sh"` | `bash` | `hooks/strategy-rotation.sh` ✅ |
| `bash "${...}/done-criteria.sh"` | `bash` | `hooks/done-criteria.sh` ✅ |
| `CLAUDE_HOOK_EVENT=SessionStart ${...}/telemetry-posthog.sh` | `CLAUDE_HOOK_EVENT=SessionStart` | `hooks/telemetry-posthog.sh` ✅ |
| `CLAUDE_HOOK_EVENT=SessionEnd ${...}/telemetry-posthog.sh` | `CLAUDE_HOOK_EVENT=SessionEnd` | `hoo

items.content:
## Summary

- Problem: `pnpm build` fails on Windows when the runtime staging step tries to create a file symlink and Node returns `EPERM` or `EACCES`.
- Why it matters: this blocks local builds on Windows environments where file symlink creation is restricted, such as when Developer Mode is disabled or the process is not elevated.
- What changed: added a Windows-specific fallback to copy the file when file symlink creation is denied, and updated the related test coverage.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): directory symlink behavior, non-Windows behavior, and the broader build pipeline were not changed.

items.author: le0nardomartins

items.title: Plugin: add proactive context usage alerts with one-tap compact
items.content:
## Summary

Sends a proactive warning when context remaining drops below 25% and a critical alert below 10% after each turn. Each alert includes a one-tap "Compact Now" button. Alerts fire once per threshold crossing and reset after compaction.

## Why this matters

Long-running Codex threads silently approach the context window limit. The `ContextUsageSnapshot` data is already available after every turn (stored in `StoredBinding.contextUsage`), but there's no proactive alert surface. Users discover the limit only when a turn fails or output quality drops.

This builds on the "push notification" direction in [#25](https://github.com/pwrdrvr/openclaw-codex-app-server/pull/25) (monitor channels).

## Changes

- **`src/types.ts`**: Added `ContextAlertLevel` type, `lastContextAlertLevel` field on `StoredBinding`, and threshold constants (`CONTEXT_ALERT_WARNING_PERCENT = 25`, `CONTEXT_ALERT_CRITICAL_PERCENT = 10`)
- **`src/format.ts`**: Added `formatContextUsageAlert()` that reuses `formatCodexContextUsageSnapshot()` for the usage text and prefixes level-appropriate messaging
- **`src/controller.ts`**: Added `checkContextUsageAlert()` private method called after each turn completion. Also resets `lastContextAlertLevel` to `null` after successful compaction so alerts can fire again if context refills
- **`src/format.test.ts`**: Three tests covering warning, critical, and empty-snapshot formatting

Alert deduplication: `lastContextAlertLevel` on the binding tracks what was last alerted. Same-level alerts are suppressed. Downgrade from critical to warning is also suppressed (already alerted at a higher severity). Crossing above the warning threshold resets the level to `null`.

The "Compact Now" button uses `store.putCallback({ kind: "run-prompt", prompt: "/cas_compact" })` to reuse the existing compact infrastructure.

## Video Demo

![Demo](https://files.catbox.moe/hz9jwa.gif)

## Testing

- `pnpm test` - 197 tests pass (3 new for `formatContextUsageAlert`)
- `pnpm typech

items.author: brunobuddy

items.title: Status: surface memory probe errors instead of hiding them
items.content:
## Problem
When `/status` runs, it calls `manager.probeVectorAvailability()` to check memory health but **silently swallows all errors** with an empty catch block. If the probe fails (network issue, misconfiguration, permission error), users see stale or misleading status output with no indication that the probe didn't run.

## Change
- Capture probe errors as a string
- Add optional `probeError` field to `MemoryStatusSnapshot`
- Display probe errors in the memory status line with `warn()` formatting

## User Impact
**Before:** Silent failure; users debugging memory issues had no visibility into probe failures  
**After:** `/status` shows: `Memory: X files · Y chunks · probe error: <message>`

Helps users diagnose memory/search issues by surfacing hidden failures.

## Verification
- Type-checks pass
- Logic review: error captured, threaded through types, displayed with warn()
- No functional tests added (status command lacks test coverage for this path)

items.title: fix(kiloclaw): backfill Stream Chat on redeploy for pre-existing instances
items.content:
## Summary

- Stream Chat was only provisioned during the first `provision()` call due to an `isNew` guard. Instances created before the feature was added (or where initial setup failed) never got Stream Chat credentials, so clicking "Redeploy or Upgrade" produced `openclaw.json` without `channels.streamchat`.
- Replaced the `isNew` gate in `provision()` with a `!streamChatApiKey` check so both first-provision and re-provision trigger Stream Chat setup when credentials are missing.
- Added the same backfill in `restartMachineInBackground()` (the "Redeploy or Upgrade" code path) before `buildUserEnvVars()` so the credentials are available when building the machine env vars.
- `setupDefaultStreamChatChannel()` is idempotent (upsert users, getOrCreate channel) and failure remains non-fatal in both paths.

## Verification

- [x] `pnpm typecheck` — 0 errors
- [x] `pnpm test` — 1054 tests passed (301 in the instance test file, including 9 new Stream Chat backfill tests)
- [x] `pnpm lint` — 0 warnings, 0 errors

## Visual Changes

N/A

## Reviewer Notes

- The backfill runs in two places: `provision()` (covers platform re-provision) and `restartMachineInBackground()` (covers user "Redeploy or Upgrade"). Both are non-fatal — if the Stream Chat API is down, the instance still starts/restarts without chat support.
- Reconciliation backfill was considered but not added to avoid periodic Stream Chat API load. The restart + re-provision paths cover the user-facing action.
- The `setupDefaultStreamChatChannel` function was already idempotent by design (reactivates users on re-provision after destroy, upserts users, getOrCreate channel), so calling it on existing instances is safe.

items.author: Copilot

items.title: Research: clean-room PPTX/presentation generation crate
items.content:
## Context

Current presentation generation workflow uses either python-pptx (mature but limited) or Aspose.Slides FOSS (MIT, pure Python, newer but incomplete API). Both are external dependencies with trade-offs — python-pptx lacks advanced styling, Aspose FOSS is early and API-incomplete, commercial Aspose requires .NET runtime + OpenSSL compat hacks on modern Linux.

The pattern: scout excellent OSS for architecture → clean-room rewrite to own standards. Same as CozoDB → mneme, OpenClaw → aletheia.

## Proposal

Research feasibility of a Rust crate (possibly `theatron-slides` or `poiesis`) that generates OOXML PPTX files natively:

- Pure Rust, no .NET/Python/Java runtime
- PPTX is just ZIP + XML — the format is well-documented (ECMA-376)
- Scope: slides, text frames, shapes, tables, images, charts, speaker notes, master slides
- Not a full PowerPoint clone — focused on programmatic generation for reports, dashboards, presentations
- Integrates with aletheia's report generation pipeline (currently typst for PDF)
- Could serve as the presentation output format for ergon_tools report system

## Prior Art to Study

- [python-pptx](https://github.com/scanny/python-pptx) — Python, MIT, mature. Study its OOXML abstraction patterns.
- [Aspose.Slides FOSS](https://github.com/nicklarosa/Aspose.Slides-for-Python-FOSS) — Python, MIT, newer. Study API design.
- [rust-pptx](https://crates.io/search?q=pptx) — check if any Rust crates exist
- [docx-rs](https://github.com/cstkingkey/docx-rs) — Rust DOCX generation. Similar problem space, study patterns.
- ECMA-376 spec — the OOXML standard

## Success Criteria

- Generate a valid PPTX that opens in PowerPoint, LibreOffice, Google Slides
- Text formatting (font, size, color, bold, alignment)
- Shapes (rectangles, rounded rectangles, lines, circles)
- Tables with cell formatting
- Embedded images (PNG/JPEG)
- Speaker notes
- Master slides / layouts
- No unsafe code, no external runtime dependencies
items.author: forkwright

items.title: fix(desktop): harden lifecycle robustness — external runner, evidence-based updates, unified teardown
items.content:
## What

Comprehensive hardening of the desktop app's startup, shutdown, and update lifecycle to eliminate several classes of real-world failures.

## Why

- **"App is in use" during reinstall**: launchd services mmap'd Electron Framework from inside .app bundle, locking it for Finder
- **Stale service attach after version upgrade**: no version/identity check on attach, causing new Electron to connect to old controller
- **Dev mode stale services**: closing window or Cmd+Q in dev mode skipped launchd teardown
- **Dirty installs**: update proceeded regardless of whether processes were actually holding critical file handles
- **Infinite restart loops**: daemon-supervisor had no circuit breaker

## How

### External Node Runner (core fix for "app in use")
- APFS-clone Electron binary + Frameworks to `~/.nexu/runtime/nexu-runner.app/`
- APFS-clone controller sidecar to `~/.nexu/runtime/controller-sidecar/`
- OpenClaw sidecar already extracted by existing logic
- All extractions use **staging dir + atomic rename** to prevent half-copies
- Version-stamped; re-extracts on app update
- Falls back to in-bundle paths if extraction fails (disk full, permissions)

### Evidence-Based Update Install
- Two sweeps of SIGKILL (15s + 5s) using both launchd labels and pgrep
- Then `lsof +D` check on critical paths (.app bundle, runner, sidecars)
- Decision matrix:
  - No processes, no locks → install
  - Processes alive, no critical locks → install (harmless residual)
  - Critical paths locked → **abort**, electron-updater retries next launch

### Version-Aware Attach
- `runtime-ports.json` now stores `appVersion`, `userDataPath`, `buildSource`, `openclawStateDir`
- Attach refused if any identity field mismatches (conservative: missing field = mismatch)
- Stale session detection: dead Electron PID + metadata > 5min → auto-bootout

### Unified Teardown
- Dev mode: window close, Cmd+Q, and `app.quit()` all run `teardownLaunchdServices`
- Packaged mode: no-window branch (renderer crash)
items.author: lefarcen

items.title: LocalLlmClient.checkAvailability() does not send auth headers, causing silent fallback when server requires API key
items.content:
## Bug Description

`LocalLlmClient.checkAvailability()` calls `fetchWithTimeout(healthUrl)` **without any authentication headers**. When the local LLM server requires API key verification (e.g., LM Studio with auth enabled, oMLX, or any OpenAI-compatible server with `Authorization: Bearer` required), the health check fails with 401 Unauthorized.

This causes `isAvailable` to be set to `false`, and **all subsequent `chatCompletion()` calls silently fall back to the gateway's default cloud LLM** — even though the actual chat completion would succeed (it does send auth headers via `buildRequestHeaders()`).

## Expected Behavior

`checkAvailability()` should send the same `Authorization` header that `chatCompletion()` uses, so that health checks pass when the server requires authentication.

## Actual Behavior

- Health check → no auth → 401 → `isAvailable = false`
- `chatCompletion()` is never attempted
- Extraction continues via fallback cloud LLM, user is unaware

## Root Cause

In `LocalLlmClient`:

```js
// checkAvailability() — line ~3208
const result = await this.fetchWithTimeout(healthUrl);
// No headers passed ↑

// chatCompletion() — works correctly
headers: this.buildRequestHeaders({ "Content-Type": "application/json" })
// Includes Authorization header ↑
```

The `buildRequestHeaders()` method constructs the `Authorization: Bearer <localLlmApiKey>` header, but it's only used in `chatCompletion()`, not in `checkAvailability()`.

## Impact

- **High**: Silent fallback to cloud LLM defeats the purpose of using a local LLM for privacy/cost
- **Deceptive**: `extractionCount` keeps incrementing, making it appear that local LLM is being used
- No error logged — `isAvailable = false` is only logged at `debug` level

## Environment

- openclaw-engram: v9.1.6
- Local LLM servers affected: Any OpenAI-compatible server with API key verification enabled
- Confirmed with: LM Studio (with auth), oMLX

## Suggested Fix

Pass auth headers in `checkAvailability()`:

```js
co

items.content:
Hi, thanks for the great tool — OpenClaw integration is very useful 🙌

I’m running into a limitation when trying to use **Windows CC-Switch + WSL OpenClaw together**.

### Current behavior

* OpenClaw (running inside WSL) reads config from:

  ```
  ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json
  ```
* CC-Switch (running on Windows) appears to manage OpenClaw providers, but:

  * It does **not expose a configurable path** for OpenClaw config (like it does for Claude/Codex/Gemini)
  * It likely reads/writes its own environment or default path (unclear)

### Problem

Because of this, I cannot make CC-Switch control the **actual config used by OpenClaw in WSL**.

Typical setup:

* OpenClaw (WSL):

  ```
  /home/<user>/.openclaw/openclaw.json
  ```
* Windows-accessible path:

  ```
  \\wsl$\Ubuntu\home\<user>\.openclaw\openclaw.json
  ```

But currently there is no way to point CC-Switch to this path.

### Expected behavior

It would be very helpful if CC-Switch could:

1. Support **custom OpenClaw config directory / file path**, similar to:

   * Claude config dir override
   * Codex config dir override

2. Example:

   ```bash
   cc-switch --app openclaw config set-path \\wsl$\Ubuntu\home\<user>\.openclaw
   ```

   or via UI:

   * “OpenClaw config directory”

3. Then CC-Switch should directly read/write:

   ```
   openclaw.json
   ```

   at that location

### Why this matters

This is important for:

* WSL-based development workflows (recommended by OpenClaw docs)
* Cross-environment setups (Windows UI + Linux runtime)
* Keeping a **single source of truth** for providers

Without this, users end up with:

* CC-Switch modifying one config
* OpenClaw using another

### Workarounds

Currently the only reliable workaround is:

* Install and run `cc-switch` inside WSL

But this removes the benefit of Windows UI / desktop integration.

### Suggestion

Add OpenClaw to the same “config override” system already used for:

* Claude
* Codex
* Gemini

---

Happy to help test if this feature is a

items.content:
Adds .github/workflows/agentshift-sync.yml and docs/github-action.md

## What's included

### `.github/workflows/agentshift-sync.yml`
A GitHub Actions workflow that:
- Triggers on push to main/master when any `SKILL.md` changes (path filter)
- Also supports `workflow_dispatch` with configurable inputs
- Finds all `SKILL.md` files (excluding `tests/`)
- Runs `agentshift convert --from openclaw --to <targets> <skill_dir> --output <skill_dir>/<output_dir>`
- Commits and pushes generated configs back to the branch via `github-actions[bot]`
- On failure: opens a GitHub Issue with details and links to logs

### Configurable workflow_dispatch inputs
| Input | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `targets` | `all` | Target platforms (comma-separated or 'all') |
| `output_dir` | `agentshift-output` | Output subdirectory name |
| `skill_pattern` | `**/SKILL.md` | Glob to find skill files |

### `docs/github-action.md`
Companion documentation covering:
- What the action does
- How to use it in your own repo
- Configuration options table
- Example output structure
- Failure handling explanation

## Tests
All 427 existing tests pass — no regressions.
items.author: ogkranthi

items.title: [Bug] External skills are ready but not injected into multi-agent specialist runtime in v2026.3.24
items.content:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

In v2026.3.24, external skills marked ready are not injected into specialist agents’ runtime prompt in multi-agent setups.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Install OpenClaw v2026.3.24 and create a multi-agent setup with `main` plus at least one specialist agent (for example `investor`).
2. Install external skills such as `engram`, `summarize`, `web-content-fetcher`, and `web-search` under `~/.openclaw/skills`.
3. Also install the same skills under the specialist agent workspace, e.g. `~/.openclaw/workspace-investor/skills`.
4. Run `openclaw skills list` and confirm those external skills are shown as ready.
5. Run a fresh-session local test such as:
   `openclaw agent --agent investor --local --session-id repro-skill-investor-1 --message "Only list the currently visible skill names." --json`
6. Inspect `systemPromptReport.skills.entries` in the JSON output.

### Expected behavior

The external skills should appear in `systemPromptReport.skills.entries` for the specialist agent when they are installed under either `~/.openclaw/skills` or the agent’s own `<workspace>/skills`.

### Actual behavior

`openclaw skills list` shows the external skills as ready, but the specialist agent runtime only receives bundled skills in `systemPromptReport.skills.entries`. The external skills (`engram`, `summarize`, `web-content-fetcher`, `web-search`) do not appear.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.24

### Operating system

macOS 15.4

### Install method

npm global

### Model

openai/gpt-5.4

### Provider / routing chain

openclaw -> PackyAPI -> openai/gpt-5.4

### Additional provider/model setup details

Model/auth was working normally through PackyAPI. `main` and specialist agents could successfully call `openai/gpt-5.4`, so this does not appear to be an auth, provider, or network failure. The problem only affected external skill injection into specialist runtime.

### Logs, screens
items.author: IanYang868

items.title: feat: add reusable Claude Code Review, Security Review, and Interactive workflows
items.content:
## Summary

Adds three reusable workflow definitions to this repo that any `georgian-io` repository can call with a two-line caller stub.

## Workflows

### `claude-code-review.yaml`
- Triggers on every PR (`opened`, `synchronize`)
- Runs a structured review: summary, ✅ good, ⚠️ warnings, ❌ blockers, security assessment by risk level
- Updates an existing review comment in-place (idempotent)

### `claude-security-review.yaml`
- Triggers on every PR — separate from code review
- Focused exclusively on: hardcoded secrets, injection, auth/authz, IAM, unpinned CI actions, PII, supply chain
- Risk-levelled output: 🔴 Critical → 🟢 Low

### `claude-interactive.yaml`
- For repos with an OpenClaw config (`openclaw/` dir or `openclaw.json`)
- Enables `@claude` mentions in issues, PRs, and review comments
- Calls the interactive `claude-code-action`

## Usage

Caller stub (2 lines, all callers look like this):

```yaml
jobs:
  claude-code-review:
    uses: georgian-io/global-github-actions-public/.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yaml@main
    secrets:
      CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
```

## Rollout

After this merges, run the `deploy-claude-workflows` workflow in `georgian-claude-code-skills` to open PRs across all repos automatically.

## Secret requirement ⚠️

Repos need `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`. **Recommend promoting to an org-level secret** so all repos inherit it without per-repo configuration.

items.title: Add regression coverage for RSVP updates inside `calendar.html` day-detail modal
items.content:
## Test Coverage Gap

- Workflow Focus: Calendar
- Type: targeted test gap

### Workflow
Calendar

### Risk Summary
Parents and coaches can open a date in the global calendar, submit an RSVP from the day-detail modal, and still see stale button state or summary because the save path updates in-memory data but deliberately does not refresh the open modal. That is a user-facing trust break in the Calendar workflow and likely to hide regressions until production.

### Coverage Gap
`calendar.html` renders live RSVP controls inside the day-detail modal for DB-backed games and practices, but there is no targeted test that opens the calendar grid, drills into a day, submits an RSVP, and verifies the modal updates without forcing the user to close and reopen it.

### Suggested Tests
1. Playwright scenario: load `calendar.html` with a DB-backed game on a visible month day, open the day modal, click `Going`, assert `submitRsvp` received the expected `teamId`, `gameId`, `currentUserId`, and parent `playerIds`, then verify the modal reflects the selected RSVP state and updated summary before closing.
2. Regression scenario: in calendar view, open a day containing both a DB-backed event and an ICS-only event, confirm only the DB-backed event shows actionable RSVP buttons, submit `Maybe` on the DB-backed event, and verify the modal stays open with refreshed availability text while the ICS event remains non-actionable.

### Code Evidence
- `/home/paul-bot1/.openclaw/workspace/allplays/calendar.html:181 `renderRsvpBlock` injects RSVP controls for DB-backed game/practice events, making the modal an interactive workflow rather than read-only display.`
- `/home/paul-bot1/.openclaw/workspace/allplays/calendar.html:560 `openDayDetail` builds the day-detail modal from filtered events and includes `renderRsvpBlock(ev)` for each item.`
- `/home/paul-bot1/.openclaw/workspace/allplays/calendar.html:609 `submitCalendarRsvp` updates `allEvents` and reruns `applyFilters()`, but the calendar-vie
items.author: pauljsnider

items.title: docs: AGENTS.md — AI agent integration guide
items.content:
## Overview

Create an AGENTS.md that helps AI agents (OpenClaw, Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) use the Partiful CLI effectively without reading every help page.

## Proposed Content

1. **Capability summary** — What the CLI can and can't do
2. **Common workflows** — Step-by-step flows:
   - "Create event and invite friends" (contacts search → create → invite)
   - "Check who's coming" (events list → guests list)
   - "Send a text blast" (events list → blasts send)
   - "Clone a recurring event" (events clone)
3. **Decision tree** — Route from user intent to the right command
4. **Schema reference** — Point agents to `partiful schema <path>` for parameter details
5. **Auth status check** — Always run `partiful doctor` or `partiful auth status` first
6. **Error recovery** — Common errors (403 permission, missing userId, upload failures) and fixes
7. **Gotchas** — Things agents get wrong:
   - Contacts don't expose emails/phone numbers (privacy)
   - Guest lists only work for events you host
   - Image upload may fail; use `--poster` or `--poster-search` as fallback

## Format

Follow the emerging AGENTS.md convention (used by Cursor, Claude Code, OpenClaw).
items.author: KalebCole

items.title: feat(kanban): support custom column keys via board config
items.content:
## Summary

Closes #172

Widens the kanban `TaskStatus` type from a hard-coded 6-value enum to a validated kebab-case string, allowing users to define custom columns (e.g. `blocked`, `testing`, `deployed`) through the existing `PUT /api/kanban/config` endpoint without any code changes.

## What changed

### Server
- **`kanban-store.ts`** — `TaskStatus` is now `string`; `BUILT_IN_STATUSES` const exported for reference; `normalizeTaskStatus()` accepts custom keys present in the live board config; `STATUS_ORDER` falls back to 99 for unknown keys
- **`routes/kanban.ts`** — `taskStatusSchema` replaced from `z.enum()` to `z.string().regex()` (lowercase-kebab-case, max 64 chars)

### Client
- **`hooks/useKanban.ts`** — fetches `/api/kanban/config` on mount; exposes `boardColumns` and `boardConfig`; `statusCounts` uses `Record<string, number>`
- **`KanbanBoard.tsx`** — accepts optional `boardColumns` prop; passes `activeColumns` to `useKanbanDragDrop`
- **`KanbanColumn.tsx`** — accepts optional `label` prop; auto-title-cases unknown keys; safe tone fallback
- **`KanbanPanel.tsx`** — passes `boardColumns` from `useKanban` down to board
- **`hooks/useKanbanDragDrop.ts`** — accepts `activeColumns` option instead of static `COLUMNS`
- **`tone.ts`** — `TASK_STATUS_TONE` key widened to `string`

## Backwards compatibility

All six built-in statuses work exactly as before. Existing boards are unaffected.

## Usage

```bash
curl -X PUT /api/kanban/config -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{
  "columns": [
    {"key": "backlog",     "title": "Backlog",     "visible": true},
    {"key": "todo",        "title": "To Do",       "visible": true},
    {"key": "in-progress", "title": "In Progress", "visible": true},
    {"key": "blocked",     "title": "Blocked",     "visible": true},
    {"key": "review",      "title": "Review",      "visible": true},
    {"key": "done",        "title": "Done",        "visible": true},
    {"key": "cancelled",   "title": "Cancelled",   "visible": fals

### What it covers

A complete AI SDR (Sales Development Representative) for B2B trade companies:
- 7-layer context system: IDENTITY, SOUL, AGENTS, USER, HEARTBEAT, MEMORY, TOOLS
- Full pipeline: Lead capture → Qualification → Needs analysis → Product matching → Quotation → Follow-up → Close
- Multi-channel (WhatsApp + Telegram + email) with unified context
- 10 automated cron jobs (pipeline reviews, follow-up reminders, weekly reports)
- Smart lead scoring by country, industry, order size

Deployed with Chinese export companies (manufacturers, trading companies) selling to 50+ countries. The 7-layer context architecture was iterated through real sales conversations.

items.title: Add use case: WordPress B2B Site Deployment
items.content:
## New Use Case

**Category:** Infrastructure & DevOps

**WordPress B2B Site Deployment** — Deploy a production-ready WordPress trade site with Docker, SSL, multilingual support, and triple-layer caching, fully guided by an OpenClaw agent over SSH.

### What it covers

An interactive 9-phase workflow where the agent:
1. Collects business requirements (company, products, target markets, languages)
2. Provisions and hardens a Linux server
3. Deploys a Docker Compose stack (WordPress 6.7 + MySQL 8 + Nginx)
4. Configures SSL (Let's Encrypt or Cloudflare Origin)
5. Installs and configures 9 curated plugins via WP-CLI
6. Sets up multilingual content with Polylang
7. Configures Rank Math SEO with company schema
8. Enables triple-layer caching (WP Super Cache + Nginx + Cloudflare)
9. Hardens security and runs a final verification checklist

### Based on real experience

Extracted from a production deployment at [TitanPuls.com](https://titanpuls.com) (semi-trailer manufacturer, 50+ countries). Template and docs: [iPythoning/wordpress-trade-starter](https://github.com/iPythoning/wordpress-trade-starter).

### Why Infrastructure & DevOps

The use case is primarily about server provisioning, Docker orchestration, SSL automation, and cache layer configuration — all executed by the agent via SSH. The WordPress/B2B context provides the business logic layer on top.
